items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander ready to shoulder another champion run after incredible Game 3 win over Suns
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander ready to shoulder another champion run after incredible Game 3 win over Suns — Gilgeous-Alexander had arguably the best games of his already-prolific playoff career in Saturday's 121-109 win.

items.headline: Injuries to Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo will test Timberwolves' depth as they aim to make third straight WCF trip
items.blurb: Injuries to Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo will test Timberwolves' depth as they aim to make third straight WCF trip — The depth of the Timberwolves has shone throughout their first-round playoff series against the Denver Nuggets. They'll now have to rely on it even more.

items.headline: Jokic’s triple-double helps Nuggets stave off elimination vs. Timberwolves
items.blurb: Jokic’s triple-double helps Nuggets stave off elimination vs. Timberwolves — Nikola Jokic snapped out of his prolonged funk with a triple-double, Spencer Jones provided a key spark and the Denver Nuggets staved off elimination with a chippy 125-113 win over the injury-riddled Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 5 of their playoff series Monday night.

items.headline: Desmond Bane stars in front of his former teammates in Game 4 win
items.blurb: Desmond Bane stars in front of his former teammates in Game 4 win — Desmond Bane is no longer a member of the Memphis Grizzlies. But playing in front of former teammates Ja Morant and Jaren Jackson, Jr., Bane was the star in a "grit-n-grind" style game, complete with bleeding arms.

items.headline: Johnson throws down massive dunk to cap off Nuggets massive third quarter
items.blurb: Johnson throws down massive dunk to cap off Nuggets massive third quarter — Watch as Denver Nuggets’ Cameron Johnson throws down a massive dunk to cap off his team’s massive third quarter, extending their lead to 25 points over the Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 4.

items.headline: Ex-NBA player Damon Jones set to plead guilty in gambling sweep
items.blurb: Ex-NBA player Damon Jones set to plead guilty in gambling sweep — Former NBA player and assistant coach Damon Jones is set to plead guilty Tuesday to charges he cashed in on rigged poker games and gave sports bettors nonpublic injury information about stars, including his one-time teammate LeBron James.

items.headline: Magic’s Wagner to undergo MRI after sustaining calf injury
items.blurb: Magic’s Wagner to undergo MRI after sustaining calf injury — The German Forward exited the Orlando Magic’s Game 4 win over the Detroit Pistons with 1:34 remaining in the third quarter and did not return due to right calf soreness.

items.headline: Heat's big changes won't include Pat Riley or Bam Adebayo
items.blurb: Heat's big changes won't include Pat Riley or Bam Adebayo — The Miami Heat know they need to make changes after winning just a single playoff game in the last three seasons. Monday, team president Pat Riley confirmed those changes wouldn't involve Heat center Bam Adebayo — or himself.

items.headline: Thunder complete sweep of Suns, advance to second round
items.blurb: Thunder complete sweep of Suns, advance to second round — The Thunder became the first team to advance to the second round of the NBA playoffs after completing a sweep of the Phoenix Suns with a 131-122 win on Monday night.

items.headline: Oklahoma City Thunder sweep their way to second round
items.blurb: Oklahoma City Thunder sweep their way to second round — Facing elimination, the Phoenix Suns didn't lie down against the Oklahoma City Thunder. It wasn't enough to avoid a four-game sweep.

items.headline: Magic one win away from upset of top-seed Pistons
items.blurb: Magic one win away from upset of top-seed Pistons — Desmond Bane scored 22 points, Franz Wagner had 19 in three quarters and the Orlando Magic beat the Detroit Pistons 94-88 Monday night to take a 3-1 series lead, putting the East’s No. 1 seed on the brink of elimination.

items.blurb: NBA, FIBA going through bids from potential owners for European league — The NBA and FIBA are in the process of going through bids from those seeking franchises for a new league in Europe, with multiple potential ownership groups from all 12 of the league’s targeted cities expressing interest.
